My girls are outgrowing the clothes and pajamas they have here at my house. So how do their stepmom and I solve this? Do we just go buy some? Nope. Joann had flannel on sale, so we bought fabric for six bucks per nightgown/outfit, and bam, the girls will each have two nights’ worth here. Haha

Carrie 8/1/2022 7:10 pm
Ex’s wife and I decided that preK online learning is just… not plausible. There is no way these girls are gonna learn anything remotely, and it sucks so bad for them. I cannot even imagine how kids in the later grades are faring.
AsyaB 9/1/2022 1:04 am
Pre K is impossible for virtual..ouch. It's tough when it's only one week of virtual and they're out of their flow
Jenavive018 9/1/2022 1:08 am
I don't have kids but if pre k was gonna be virtual I'd just find an educational kids show for free (or that I'm already paying for the platform it's on) My best friend had a K and I think 2nd grader in online classes and she says it was hell (edited)
sincerely.sarina 9/1/2022 1:22 am
I co-parent 50/50 and Cayden, my son, is 3. I want to get him in preschool but our childcare is in 2 different cities a fair distance apart and I don’t think it’s fair to Cayden to have to go to separate preschools and childcare for each household. His dad refuses to communicate with me (blocked my ability to text, hangs up when he believes I’m “getting worked up”, or just won’t answer if he thinks I’m angry, upset, etc). I don’t even want to think about schooling and how it may affect my job but I don’t want Cayden to be behind because he missed pre-K. I hope all this makes sense lol (edited)
1:25 am
When I was in college, I was in ROTC and I missed all the “pre-events” because I had to work and honestly that’s the only thing I can really “compare experiences” to. I don’t want him to fall behind and feel less than because his dad and I can’t get on the same page. (edited)
sincerely.sarina
When I was in college, I was in ROTC and I missed all the “pre-events” because I had to work and honestly that’s the only thing I can really “compare experiences” to. I don’t want him to fall behind and feel less than because his dad and I can’t get on the same page. (edited)
Jenavive018 9/1/2022 1:34 am
That sounds like an incredibly frustrsting situation to try and be co raising a little human being in... (Again need to preface I don't have my own kids. My mom did childcare for 20 years though and I helped when I was older) But I do want to add pre K doesn't necessarily determine if someone is "behind" or not. Both my older brothers went and I didn't. I was always in the talented and gifted program (along with 1 brother, the other is autistic). Both brothers (and now my baby brother who also didn't have pre K) took an extra year of high school while I did not. I took college courses in high school and was the first in my family to get a degree I def see the benefits of it but if it won't work for your situation don't consider it an end all our a detriment to your son's learning. I'm not sure how his day cares are but being with other kids is definitely helpful and if they do any constructive time (crafts etc) they often benefit from that I hope for his sake you and your ex can form a better communication by the time he's in school
Jenavive018
That sounds like an incredibly frustrsting situation to try and be co raising a little human being in... (Again need to preface I don't have my own kids. My mom did childcare for 20 years though and I helped when I was older) But I do want to add pre K doesn't necessarily determine if someone is "behind" or not. Both my older brothers went and I didn't. I was always in the talented and gifted program (along with 1 brother, the other is autistic). Both brothers (and now my baby brother who also didn't have pre K) took an extra year of high school while I did not. I took college courses in high school and was the first in my family to get a degree I def see the benefits of it but if it won't work for your situation don't consider it an end all our a detriment to your son's learning. I'm not sure how his day cares are but being with other kids is definitely helpful and if they do any constructive time (crafts etc) they often benefit from that I hope for his sake you and your ex can form a better communication by the time he's in school

sincerely.sarina 11/1/2022 1:13 pm

Carrie 8/2/2022 1:18 pm
We do have my five year old in the process of intake papers, for her to be tested for autism with Easter Seals, but the wait time after receiving those papers is ten to twelve months.
Livethemoment 8/2/2022 2:15 pm
Oh no! Chewing can get so bad. I've seen a client chew through a woody toy hat, it wasn't small, in a matter of a few days. Diagnosis take a long time and I'm sure it sucks. Though for a program, that seems long. Though where you live may have stricker guidelines than where I live. The only time we have to waitlist for one of the services we offer is when we need to hire more staff, but they can usually get started on receiving similar services. I can only imagine how hard that process is.
Carrie 8/2/2022 2:21 pm
Easter Seals is the program taking so long. I think it has been a combo of panini, and that they cover a large area of the state.
Carrie

Carrie 24/4/2022 1:35 am
Just got the draft for A’s 42 page long IEP/evaluation for kindergarten. Kinda near tears, because they are finally noticing the “quirks” we have been observing, and the next step is happening to get her diagnosed with autism. It was simultaneously heartbreaking and hopeful, because to hear that she is struggling was rough, but they have a plan for her, to help her learn.
Carrie
Just got the draft for A’s 42 page long IEP/evaluation for kindergarten. Kinda near tears, because they are finally noticing the “quirks” we have been observing, and the next step is happening to get her diagnosed with autism. It was simultaneously heartbreaking and hopeful, because to hear that she is struggling was rough, but they have a plan for her, to help her learn.
TanyaElisabeth 24/4/2022 6:34 am
I’m glad they have a plan. And I understand how you feel about the diagnosis. But I wouldn’t change anything for Sean. He’s got Autism and d Aspergers.
1
Angel (he/they) 24/4/2022 6:39 am
As an autistic adult, it's really good that you're getting her diagnosed now. It's a lot harder to figure it out on your own.
2
Livethemoment 24/4/2022 7:42 am
I believe it. I know for parents it can be really hard and difficult, but I've seen how it is helpful.
Carrie 24/4/2022 7:58 am

Carrie
I am not so much upset about her diagnosis, because it just means she is wired different and we need to adjust our parenting techniques. I think it was just knowing that she has been struggling.
Livethemoment 24/4/2022 7:59 am

I have a question for y'all.
10:56 am
OK toddlers excluded .. Why do y'all kids stare? It's creepy AF. I'm sure your kids great n all but why?
Angel (he/they) 18/6/2022 10:56 am
Not a parent but they're curious and they don't know better. 0 social skills bc they're literally too young to know
10:57 am
I get it a ton with blue hair
Catherine 18/6/2022 10:59 am

Angel (he/they) 18/6/2022 10:59 am

Livethemoment 18/6/2022 11:04 am
I echo what Angel said. They also may not have been taught how it is inappropriate. Social rules are definitely hard and a bit too abstract for most kids. Catherine 18/6/2022 11:04 am

Tami sue 8/7/2022 10:49 am
I could use some advice. My 3.5 y/o grandson has been potty trained for at least six months, and he just became a big brother last month and has decided to poop his pants and wants to wear diapers . My daughter has been doing great giving him plenty of attention but also has the baby (who was a month early) to deal with so she relented and is putting him in diapers. He is coming tomorrow to spend the weekend with us and I want to help him the best I can…. Anyone dealt with this???
Livethemoment 8/7/2022 11:10 am
@TamiH Maybe a bathroom schedule where he goes every couple of hours or so? You could maybe have a sticker chart where he earns a sticker and is provided verbal praise every time he uses the bathroom. Then after getting so many stickers he can get a bigger reward. (edited)
3
11:11 am
I've also taken the time to pair going to the bathroom with preferred things like maybe watching 1 or 2 minutes of a favorite show or something like that
3
Tami sue 8/7/2022 11:14 am
Thank you!!
Livethemoment 8/7/2022 11:15 am
You are welcome. I have plenty of options . I haven't dealt with this exact situation, but have had to work with kids on using the bathroom.
1
Carrie 10/7/2022 2:22 am
Well, I did not want to discuss it in there, but my youngest, S4, has become “obsessed” with me, beyond an age appropriate clinginess. While her sister was at school, she told me she never wanted A5 to come home, so it could be just me and her. She spends her whole day glued to me, and kinda “claims” me and fights with her sister over me. Her dad and stepmother are concerned, especially about the comments about not wanting A5 around. It started right when the schedule switch started (he has weekdays during the school year, and I have weekdays during the summer), so we are unsure what is driving the behavior. We plan to discuss it with her preschool the moment we can get in contact with them (it likely would fall under social emotional, so we are hoping we can let her see someone who pinpoint the behavior).
Angel (he/they) 10/7/2022 2:51 am
Oh thats a tough one. Is therapy an option?

LinnWI 11/7/2022 6:52 am
Sounds like an excellent plan! I did not do any of that - ended up with slime on the wall, in the carpet, in the couch, and all over the bed.. not my room, the kid’s room he got slime at Christmas, and I’m still finding remains of it here and there…
1
Carrie 17/7/2022 5:01 am
I am having such a difficult, angry, bad mom day. We cannot stay cooped up here in this apartment all day, so I wanted to take my girls swimming, but, of course… this. This property took over a month into the season to actually open the pool. It has been locked after the M-F 8-5 office hours. No notices, no emails, nothing. I have reached a point where I am just going to consider it to be permanently closed for the season, but imagine having to tell S4 and A5 that. Just heartbreaking to have to realize that we had planned to swim this summer, but the complex pool that is calculated into my rent just does not actually exist.
7
camifenn 17/7/2022 5:59 am
That is miserable, especially for kids who see a perfectly good pool and need to play!
natalie. 17/7/2022 6:02 am
That sucks! Are you able to talk to management about it?
Carrie 17/7/2022 7:43 am
Oh, management and I have already butted heads. She kicked me out of the office for wearing a swimsuit inside the building. She is rather, um, militant. I cannot even stand to be in a room with her more than five minutes, if I have to. I also have to be in a headspace to talk to her.
TanyaElisabeth 23/7/2022 9:15 pm
This is gonna be a long one y'all.... So Sean has been asking about his father a lot lately...asking me questions that I truly don't have the answers to. So Sean had speech and ot on Wednesday and had a major meltdown with SIB and was asking for " my daddy". I couldn't calm him the 1st 5 minutes. Anyway, we were talking to him about how he was feeling and he said he wanted to call his father. I tried calling him, well he was in bed, allegedly. The next morning he called at 6am waking both of us up but I allowed Sean to talk to him. I laid the ground rules down. 1. This isn't a revolving door, communication has to be consistent. 2. You must answer any questions he has truthfully with no excuses. 3. The first time you don't call, this is done. Not hard right? Well, he and I had a conversation and as always, it ends up in a fight with him screaming at me that we were never a couple and I "need to get over it". He makes me so angry with his " I'm trying...I'm working on it...I have no money". This money thing has always been a battle. I have never taken him back to court for a modification. Not in 8 years. I've allowed him complete access to Sean since the beginning. Jeremy (that's his name) has been locked up the majority of Sean's life. This is the longest he's been out. Jeremy has never made an effort to come to Waco to see Sean. As petty as it may be, I refuse to bring him to Houston. We've lived in the same spot for 6 years. Never once while he was out of jail did he come
9:17 pm
So part of the blow out was me asking him for a timeline for when he was gonna come and visit, just a month of an approximation. He flipped and said, " I'm trying to better my life and if you don't believe me then that's on you". When I tell you guys that I've tried everything to help facilitate a relationship between father and son, it's gone to shit. So now here's my delema (sp). Jeremy's track record sucks for even phone calls. I don't want Sean to resent me, but I don't want Sean crushed bc Jeremy is a 1st class fuck up. (If any of you want more info on Jeremy dm me and I will explain thru voice) I know the damage of both ways of this argument. I told Jeremy the 1st call he misses, he's done and all he will have is the obligation to pay child support. (There's a story with CS as well) Jeremy brings out the worst version of myself and triggers and pokes me in the worst way. But I will put that all aside for Sean. He deserves both parents, right? I think that's the overview of what I'm dealing with right now. I am truly jealous of people who can coparent like friends or at least have effective communication and be friendly. Lastly please don't judge me too harshly, I just don't want Sean to feel abandoned again.
TanyaElisabeth 23/7/2022 11:53 pm
@Carrie what do you think?
Carrie 23/7/2022 11:58 pm
It sounds like you are doing all that you can, which is the important part. I imagine that, yes, his life might be really rough now, but there are also resources to help him, I am sure. (Well, hopefully. Texas is not really a great state. Sorry)
11:59 pm
Would maybe keeping a journal help? Putting all your feelings and what happened into writing (I would use a composition notebook) might help you work through it, PLUS be something Sean could read some day when he is much older, so he knows you did not keep him away.
12:00 am
I have been debating doing that, in general, so my daughters have a record of my memories, etc, when I eventually pass.
TanyaElisabeth 24/7/2022 12:02 am
I've thought of that...I just idk it's one of those things that what if he thinks I made this all up... developmentally sean is like 7 years old and doesn't fully understand...he just likes talking to people on the phone and listening to himself talk
3
TanyaElisabeth 24/7/2022 2:19 am

Carrie 2/10/2022 12:03 pm
Ugh. It has been a bad brain evening. The day was okay. We walked up to the mailboxes, and took identification photos (there is an app that can identify) of plants, including roses, and a pansy (violet) plant. Rose and Violet are in my girls’ names. It gave them something on which to focus, and kinda gave us more than just a simple walk. The rest of the day was quality time together, but, oof, the nearer bedtime drew, the more arguing, and the more pointless bickering. This is what happens when one decides to put only 14 months between children. I am ready for sleep.

Carrie 20/10/2022 12:09 am
There are likely organizations that will help you. Here, we have Children’s Home & Aid, and the Baby Fold, that help foster parents and kids. Children’s Home & Aid is where they do supervised visits and stuff, so it is likely you have a similar location in your area? They would definitely know the ins and outs about local resources, and what is allowed and not allowed.

4:39 am

4:39 am

TerdNugget 20/10/2022 4:39 am

Cosmic Creepers 20/10/2022 4:39 am

4:40 am

Livethemoment 20/10/2022 4:40 am
That happens to me all the time. You just get so exhausted from your emotions and others you are trying to help that you get to a point you just can't deal with them anymore and you kind of shut down. It is totally okay that it happens. Maybe he can write it down or set up a time when ypu can handle it to talk?
TerdNugget 20/10/2022 4:40 am

Cosmic Creepers 20/10/2022 4:41 am

I'm going to talk to the therapist. I've been to 2 appointments with both of us and the therapist and did notice he is kind of silent and doesn't open up
Livethemoment
That happens to me all the time. You just get so exhausted from your emotions and others you are trying to help that you get to a point you just can't deal with them anymore and you kind of shut down. It is totally okay that it happens. Maybe he can write it down or set up a time when ypu can handle it to talk?
TerdNugget 20/10/2022 4:42 am
Writing it down is a good idea. I'm going to see if he can do that
Cosmic Creepers 20/10/2022 4:43 am
How long has he been seeing the therapist?
TerdNugget 20/10/2022 4:43 am
Thank you both
4:43 am
Since the summer
4:43 am
I think around July?
Cosmic Creepers 20/10/2022 4:43 am
I’ve seen some for months- years without feeling fully able to express things
4:43 am
Yeah that fairly new and normal for a patient to still be slightly closed off
4:43 am
Or not comfortable or know how to speak about it
4:44 am
Especially because we can’t always identify what we’re feeling
3
4:44 am
So it’s hard to tell someone else how you feel when you don’t even know other than “bad”
TerdNugget 20/10/2022 4:45 am
Me too and I get it. I'm just a bit mentally tired this week and feel guilty that I don't know how to help him. I know he's been feeling overwhelmed with school so I've been taking him to Starbucks to do hw while I read. Just to switch up the environment. Hope that helps at least get rid of school stress.
4:47 am
I did tell him that is amazing he could recognize he's feeling depressed. I noticed he's been sleeping on his clean laundry which is a sign of depression to me. I'm taking my lunch to fold his laundry and make his bed. Pretty rare for me to do that.
Cosmic Creepers 20/10/2022 4:48 am
I’m sure he’ll appreciate that
Livethemoment 20/10/2022 5:10 am
Sometimes those small things really help when you are depressed.
Carrie 20/10/2022 6:47 am
@TerdNugget Have you ever shown him a feelings wheel? Maybe it would help find a way to express himself?
TerdNugget 20/10/2022 6:56 am
I haven't but I have heard about it! Thanks for the reminder!
Carrie
You are an amazing human, Tanya, and you got this. Do you use Amazon at all? Maybe make a wishlist of things we can send for help (and permission to give the list to a couple someone’s that might be able to offer some help, too?) There is a way to make the wishlist without giving your address, but you also need to open it to third parties, if you add items that do not ship from Amazon.
muttstew 20/10/2022 7:49 am
I love this idea!

Deleted User 16/2/2023 1:23 am
I have two who fight most of the time. I set goals for the day/week/month on who can treat their sibling the best , winner gets to pick a fun activity (hint everyone wins) lol. I also lost my sister 3 years ago so my oldest understands how important it is to be kind with your siblings, they're the only one you have. Mine are 10 and 6

TanyaElisabeth 16/2/2023 1:36 am

AshleyP 16/2/2023 1:38 am

Carrie 16/2/2023 2:46 am
My girls are 14 months apart, and their age really does have them at each other all the time. I very often have to separate them. Usually I shout “Separate yourselves” if it reaches a certain point. That is chance number one for them to sort it out on their own. If they do not, depending on where they are, I go over and sit between them (sometimes even walking over there fixes it ).
2:48 am
Otherwise, all three of us have decided that, unless it involves blood or hitting, we let them try to work it out. For me, I usually perk up and listen, to play mediator if it goes too far. But our interactions with siblings is one of the first places children learn conflict resolution.
2:49 am
“It is okay to be angry with your sister, but we absolutely do not hit others.” “How would you feel if S5 had done that to you?”
AshleyP 16/2/2023 2:59 am
They’re fifteen months apart and that’s exactly what I’m fighting too is the constant tattle telling

TanyaElisabeth 7/5/2023 5:30 pm
I just wrote this to seans father
5:30 pm
Dear Jeremy, Hey I see you’re back in jail. Wicked sorry that happened. NOT! Now for the reason I am writing. Ever since you stopped responding to texts and calls there has been major trauma and fallout from that for Sean. He’s currently in therapy and still has a hard time talking about how he feels without cussing and crying. This lays squarely on your shoulders. You stopped paying support And that has lead to more trauma because I cant afford to do anything extra for him. From this point, I will be going to court while you’re in jail, so you will have no choice but to show up. You can’t ignore it forever. You truly are a scum bag. You’d rather drink than be in your son’s life and that’s truly A miserable existence. I offered to meet you half way, gave you a month reprieve on support so you could come here, I have done everything but took my happy fat ass to Houston and deliver Sean Jacob on a silver platter. Jeremy, I get you don’t know how to be a parent because you were never taught how to be one. You were shipped from one bar or another or completely ignored by your own father. But you and I had many a conversation and you stating you would rather die than have Sean go through that pain. Well here we are 10 years later and you’re pulling the same shit your father did and you’re repeating the cycle. I am going to ask the judge to remove your joint legal, you’ve never exercised it anyway. I am also requesting that you be mandated to put Sean as your beneficiary to any and all VA benefits and social Security benefits you may be receiving. So he will be getting the support that he so rightfully deserves. Without you interfering or trying to control the narrative. I hope you get your life together for your own sake. I saw your mugshot and you look like hell Jeremy truly. I hope that you see the error of your ways when it comes to being a parent, its not all shitty prison tattoos. It’s about sacrifice AND loving him more than yourself or anyone else. Unfortunately I don’t think you’re built that way.
2
Angel (he/they) 8/5/2023 1:16 am
I'm so proud of you
TanyaElisabeth 8/5/2023 1:18 am
THANK YOU!
Catherine 8/5/2023 1:59 am
GOOD FOR YOU
TanyaElisabeth 8/5/2023 1:59 am
thank you
Kelsey 1/5/2024 11:09 pm
So I'm getting super anxious, we are leaving on our road trip next week. I am a single mom of 2 (5 years old with ADHD and 7 year old with severe autism) we are driving from Indiana to Virginia for my college graduation. My mom and sister, her fiance and her dog are coming with us. The trip im not too worried about we've done road trips before and done fine. It's the graduation part that I'm concerned about my 7 year old is an eloper. He likes to run away and is very fast. I'm just concerned because there will be so many people and it's a huge campus. We will have hopefully harness on them with appletags but my anxiety is still there. Plus there's the whole thing of the fact that I'm not telling their dad that we are leaving. (he lives in California and hasn't seen them in over 2 years, but would cause a stink if I mentioned a trip and it wasn't toward him). Gotta love coparenting with a toxic parent (edited)
TanyaElisabeth 1/5/2024 11:13 pm
Sean was an eloper. When he was 2 I had to fly from Massachusetts to Tx with a layover in St.Louis. I got a baby backpack with a “leash “ no such thing as AirTags back then. And if he will sit in it, use a stroller when walking around campus. I don’t even co-parent with Sean’s donor bc he took himself out of the equation. But I’m still legally required to let him know if we leave out of state and I need his signature for Sean to get a passport. Do you guys share any form custody, mainly joint legal? If you do, you may be required to tell him.
Kelsey 1/5/2024 11:14 pm
I have sole legal and primary physical he gets visitation in Indiana only and is supposed to pay child support but doesn't
TanyaElisabeth 1/5/2024 11:15 pm
Yeah Sean’s donor is in excess of 44k in arrears. Well legally you don’t have to tell him a damn thing. So don’t let that ruin your graduation
Kelsey 1/5/2024 11:16 pm
Yeah we are 7,000 right now in arrears but he's only supposed to pay 60 a week
TanyaElisabeth 1/5/2024 11:17 pm
That’s so bad. My ex and I had an agreement out of Massachusetts. I’ve been fighting Texas OAG to enforce since 2018. I have finally gotten someone who only deals with out of state agreements
11:19 pm
Which reminds me I have to call that woman and follow up and find out what’s going on
Kelsey 1/5/2024 11:20 pm
Gotta love that those interstate laws. He moved to California before we were even divorced, and they just got California involved this year. But he even told our case work that he worked last year and still chose not to pay child support even though he lives with his dad and doesn't pay any bills
TanyaElisabeth 1/5/2024 11:21 pm
Wow. My ex gets VA disability. All my ex has to do is add him as a “dependent “ and his support is covered. He won’t lose any money.
Kelsey 1/5/2024 11:23 pm
That's nice hope he does that
TanyaElisabeth 1/5/2024 11:24 pm
He won’t. He was told by his lawyer who was instructed by the judge at the time to do it and he never did. This was 10 years ago I’ve tried to get Sean added when my ex was in prison. It’s been a literal shitshow

Kelsey 1/5/2024 11:25 pm
I hate that for you
TanyaElisabeth 1/5/2024 11:25 pm

11:26 pm

Kelsey 1/5/2024 11:27 pm

TanyaElisabeth 1/5/2024 11:28 pm

alexmarie 2/5/2024 1:02 am
I have a 5 year old eloper. I use the kid leash harness on him with zip ties to double secure the buckles because he learned to undo them.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:03 am
Alexmarie I got lucky in a sense bc Sean’s fine motor skills are weak. He wasn’t ever able to get out of his car seat either.
Kelsey 2/5/2024 1:04 am
Our leash has a lock on it that the key is attached to the parent side.
1:05 am
But he's smart and learned that the key unlocked it and will try to unlock it himself so I have to keep an eye on it lol
alexmarie 2/5/2024 1:08 am
My kid has advance fine motor skills. He knows how to work the bike lock I have the fridge. Luckily he doesn't know the code.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:09 am
That’s wild
Kelsey 2/5/2024 1:09 am
Yeah my kid watched me do his parent lock passcode once on his tablet and he remembered it so I had to change it lol
alexmarie 2/5/2024 1:11 am
My son did that to his speech therapist . He saw the code and unlocked the tablet to play the speech game mid therapy.
Kelsey 2/5/2024 1:13 am
Yeah, I get notes home from my sons teacher “he was on google again instead of the school computer apps”
alexmarie
My son did that to his speech therapist . He saw the code and unlocked the tablet to play the speech game mid therapy.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:14 am
When Sean was younger we were having to change passcodes all the time. Now I have facial recognition or fingerprint.
alexmarie 2/5/2024 1:16 am
I have facial recognition on my phone and fingerprint on my tablet. My son leaves those alone along as I allow him to have a tv on when he plays. ABA therapy has done him wonders.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:18 am
ABA is awesome. But you have to make sure they’re a good fit. I made sure Sean’s therapist wasn’t like the old school method of ABA.
Kelsey 2/5/2024 1:24 am
I loved my sons ABA. My son did 3 years and was non-verbal when he started at ABA. They helped us transition to his school so well and he started in kindergarten this year part time ABA part time. He's in a transitional classroom that's for autistic kids. They call it the bridge program at his school, where he is able to still get education but still the specialized programs like speech and Ot and a bcba care. Plus they help transition social interaction by slowly moving into the normal classroom. It's really smart.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:26 am
I wish my district had a program like that...I cant even get them to allow my sons ABA therapist to work in the school where all of his SIB triggers are
1
Kelsey 2/5/2024 1:27 am
I would also suggest to check into Project Lifesaver for your eloper. They have trackers that emergency responders can use if your loved runs and get too far away and many places will change the batteries and bands and it's free for qualifying individuals. They are also waterproof
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:28 am
Sean is no longer an eloper but that sounds like an amazing program!
Kelsey 2/5/2024 1:29 am
Thats really frustrating that the school won't allow his therapist for the sib, my son also does sib and aggression towards other so I get it
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:30 am
Waco ISD is the literal worst.
Kelsey 2/5/2024 1:31 am
I don't know anything about any schools outside of Indiana. Lol so I'm learning new things
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 1:32 am
Texas is so fucked for education. I@ just rolling with it for now.
alexmarie 2/5/2024 1:54 am
I am homeschooling so my son (5yo) can be in all the therapies he is in full time until at least 2nd grade. (I live in Ohio and a SAHM) He is thriving this way and loves our learning time.
1
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 4:36 am
I wish I could have homeschooled. But even with my education background I couldn’t “reach” Sean.
Kelsey 2/5/2024 4:58 am
I am not smart enough to educate them especially with the new education they teach…and being a single parent definitely need to make money somehow without joining an mlm
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 5:21 am
That’s the other side of the coin Kelsey. The common core method of education is the main reason why I stopped my degree in education sped and early childhood psych
alexmarie 2/5/2024 5:33 am
What is nice about homeschool is you don't have to teach CCM. I went to a homeschool convention which helped me figure out how to teach my AuDHD son. Once those skills and methods were recommended and I could run with them his learning kicked into over drive and he flew.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 5:33 am
I may look into that and kind of do a home summer school so he doesn’t regress
alexmarie 2/5/2024 5:34 am
I highly recommend Evan-Moor workbooks
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 5:35 am
Okay. I’ll for sure look into it. He tends to regress over summer break and summer school through Waco ISD is a joke
Kelsey 2/5/2024 5:35 am
Nice, when my son gets older we may switch to an online school if traditional isn't working because his classroom ends after elementary.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 5:36 am
I’m so nervous for when he gets into middle school he doesn’t transition well to switching classes.
alexmarie 2/5/2024 5:37 am
My son is using Evan-Moor Skill Sharpeners: Critical Thinking, Grade PreK for extra learning time. He is enjoying it. I use all the workbooks as supplements to the curriculum I use to teach them.
5:39 am
Transitions are hard. My son doesn't transition well. We use visual timers so after all the color is gone it's time to transition to the next thing. If we were doing something we want to come back to we set it to the side. I know in schools this isn't ideal but at home to help lessen the disruption can help and the feeling/knowledge that transitions are okay can flow into the classroom.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 5:44 am
I don’t have an issue at home. All of his triggers and issues are at school.
HausOfAus 2/5/2024 6:32 am
@TanyaElisabethI just wanna say as someone who isn't a parent, I wanna commend you so so so much. You are really really trying to make Sean's life better and in turn, yours and I just wanna assure you that we see it and we see you trying your absolute best.
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 6:37 am
Thank you. Sometimes I feel like I’m not doing enough.
HausOfAus 2/5/2024 6:37 am
I figured as much, so I just wanted to remind you that we see!
TanyaElisabeth 2/5/2024 9:06 am
thank you! I appreciate that

TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 11:54 am
I dont homeschool...i tried for almost an entire year, and i couldnt reach him and so i decided to get him in school
11:55 am
and then bc i tried to educate him myself when i went and enrolled him, they wouldnt allow me to put him in kinder he had to go into 1st and ended up repeating
alexmarie 6/5/2024 11:57 am
the public school system is comical sometimes. My choices are really homeschool or private school. The public school districts I am in either have 40 student class sizes for one teacher and one helper or the school has been on lockdown for violent outbursts or weapons at least once a week.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 11:58 am
Yeah that’s all super scary. Sean has a bproof plate in his backpack
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:02 pm
My son is AuDHD and an eloper so finding out his kinder class would be 40 kids plus two adults last year I had to find a different solution for him. The goal is to homeschool until 2nd grade so he can do ABA therapy and his speech with who is already in with since he is comfortable with them and doesn't elope.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:03 pm
thats a great plan...my sons district will not allow my sons aba therapist to give him therapy in school
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:04 pm
The one district has one that works with the kids once a week for an hour. Right now he is in ABA three times a week for 2 hours a session until June then he gets bumped up to the 4 hour sessions where I leave him with the group.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:05 pm
sean only qualified for an hour a week and now bc of counseling were doing alternate weeks for aba
12:05 pm
i wish sean could do that
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:06 pm
Where I take my son they work on building them up to a full 15-20 kid group and mimic school environment so they can transition to school.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:06 pm
thats sooo cool!
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:08 pm
I love it. We started slow with 1 time a week and worked up to this now. There are 3 other kids in the 2 hour session and us parents can sit back and relax in the observation room.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:09 pm
That’s so amazing he was only able to get into the ABA that is run by Texas MHMR
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:11 pm
I wish that more people had access to Building Blocks (It's only in Ohio) or a similar program. I have seen an improvement in him at home. I am more relaxed.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:12 pm
that makes me so happy. Sean was borderline and could only go to EI for like 8 months and then I was left to flap in the breeze
12:12 pm
The speech therapist did give him the ability to talk again and I’ll be forever thankful
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:13 pm
I love our speech therapist. She has really helped him learn to control his mouth to talk. If that makes sense.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:15 pm
He’s speech therapists at our therapy center and at school are amazing.
12:15 pm
No I get what ur saying.
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:17 pm
My son goes to a Cleveland Clinic Children's Rehab and Therapy Center for Speech and OT (food therapy) every other Thursday. His off Thursdays are when mom (me) goes to psycho therapy.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:18 pm
i think seans food issues is actually ARFID not just food aversions to be conquered in OT...he was doing it for a year in therapy but would not do it at home
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:23 pm
There was a suspicion of ARFID for my son but we decided to try OT and that helped erase that. I know it is a hard thing to deal with. My cousin has ARFID, he adds supplement sprinkles to what he does eat. He is 12 now. He got himself on a schedule so he will only want to eat during those times.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:24 pm
sean is so restrictive with what he wil eat, i cant get him to eat any fruit or veg, hes supposed to be GF because along with T1D hes got celiac as well
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:25 pm
Those restrictions on ARFID is a whammy. I am pre-diabetic (PCOS) so I know those restrictions can be rough alone.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 12:26 pm
i mean in theory he can eat whatever he wants he just has to be dosed for it...but his food list keeps getting shorter...he will eat any kind of meat tho
12:26 pm
i am constantly getting hounded by his endo bc hes not on a GF diet
alexmarie 6/5/2024 12:27 pm
There are some chicken nuggets that have hidden veggies. I get them and dump them into the Tyson bag to disguise them.
TanyaElisabeth 6/5/2024 6:13 pm
ill have to try to find them
Kelsey 7/5/2024 11:27 pm
Purdue has them I think they are dino shaped?
TanyaElisabeth 8/5/2024 1:26 am

alexmarie 8/5/2024 2:26 am

alexmarie 8/5/2024 3:23 am
I GOT A MOM WIN! My AuDHD son ate a few bites of my salad. He liked it! He is still anti-tomato. But he tried lettuce, cucumber, olive, and bell pepper.

TanyaElisabeth 26/8/2024 4:00 am

Luna Mae 26/8/2024 4:04 am

TanyaElisabeth 26/8/2024 4:04 am

Luna Mae 29/9/2024 8:20 am
Yesterday was a cluster fuck of a day but in the end my son baked my cinnamon rolls and apologized for being an entitled asshole yesterday. This morning he came in my room and cuddles with me for the first time in years. We talked about yesterday and how we felt and what each of us would like to see done differently next time. He apologized again and then promptly went and did his basic chores as well as taking our dog on a long walk. He played on the computer for an hour or so and then I called him down to do a power hour of cleaning with my husband and I. Normally he complains but today he's just rolled with it. He did get irritated that I fused about him taking a break but we got over that quickly and he came back from break pretty quickly without much fighting if any at all. He finished up the power hour of cleaning and just kept going until all we have left in the main area of our house is the kitchen counters being wiped down and the hand wash dishes finished and put away. He also started a load of laundry and helped me rearrange the front room. I'm incredibly proud of Gavin for all he was able to accomplish. I feel like i want to give him an extra reward for being so awesome today that hopefully would encourage him to continue to work hard and do positive things in life. I'm just stock on what to do for that. Little back info: he still owes about $700 for back rent after today's work has been accounted for. He chose to spend his living expenses money on video games so we are trying to teach him that his actions have consequences. Hence the required power hour of cleaning. Plus the helicopter parenting of his paychecks until he can learn the habit of paying bills before buying extra stuff. He almost never goes above and beyond on anything we require our ask of him. So we are always being " negative " towards him and he equates this to him being a looser and not good enough so why should he try attitude. And I really want to change the narrative.
2
TanyaElisabeth 29/9/2024 8:35 am
I think that’s amazing that yall could have a productive conversation. I think it was a breakthrough tbh. As a “reward” for not giving u a hard time and going “above” and beyond, knock a 100 bucks off of what he owes. Don’t give him anything.
TanyaElisabeth 4/10/2024 11:45 am
Sean an I are at the local children’s hospital. He’s got a bilateral ear infection and they’re not down with giving him oral antibiotics anymore
Luna Mae 4/10/2024 12:11 pm
I'm so sorry. Are they giving him iv antibiotics?
TanyaElisabeth 4/10/2024 9:42 pm
as of 3 am...yes. we had our bed given away so were still in the er
Luna Mae 12/10/2024 12:35 pm
My son Cameron calls my husband this evening freaking out about his cat and how he needs to get him to the vet but he's broke. So my husband says that maybe we can give the cat some Tylenol and I'm like no they do gabapintin and you have to have a script for it. After googleing he finally gives up and says I'm right. So I booked an appointment with the vet and my husband takes them to the vet at 7pm. After like 1.5 hours they conclude that he needs a dental cleaning and some pain killers. We decline the dental cleaning because it's like 500$ and we just don't have that much money. So 161$ plus a case of wet food later kookie is happy and Cameron is still freaking out because he needs a dental cleaning and shots. I'm exhausted and pissed that I missed out on my date night and my son is still upset about money and the fact that I helped to get kookie the proper care. He calls us when life is falling apart and we don't hesitate to help but then he shits on me and tells everyone how horrible I am. And then praises my husband for how he has always taken care of us. But in reality I'm the one who has been there day in and day out and have bent over backwards to the point that I have made myself sick and thousands of dollars in debt to make sure they had everything they needed. And Cameron don't give a shit because I'm lower then the scum of the earth to him. Anyways rant over.
3
1

I'm so sorry. I've never been on the end of it with my kid, so I honestly don't know how I'd handle it. It's always so much harder when it's family. People always get so used to taking and then when you can't do it anymore, they get mad. I can't deal with entitlement.
Luna Mae 12/10/2024 12:41 pm

Luna Mae
My son Cameron calls my husband this evening freaking out about his cat and how he needs to get him to the vet but he's broke. So my husband says that maybe we can give the cat some Tylenol and I'm like no they do gabapintin and you have to have a script for it. After googleing he finally gives up and says I'm right. So I booked an appointment with the vet and my husband takes them to the vet at 7pm. After like 1.5 hours they conclude that he needs a dental cleaning and some pain killers. We decline the dental cleaning because it's like 500$ and we just don't have that much money. So 161$ plus a case of wet food later kookie is happy and Cameron is still freaking out because he needs a dental cleaning and shots. I'm exhausted and pissed that I missed out on my date night and my son is still upset about money and the fact that I helped to get kookie the proper care. He calls us when life is falling apart and we don't hesitate to help but then he shits on me and tells everyone how horrible I am. And then praises my husband for how he has always taken care of us. But in reality I'm the one who has been there day in and day out and have bent over backwards to the point that I have made myself sick and thousands of dollars in debt to make sure they had everything they needed. And Cameron don't give a shit because I'm lower then the scum of the earth to him. Anyways rant over.

My sister from another misters oldest is very much like your son and when my nephew trashed his mom to anyone who would listen I climbed so far up his ass he couldn’t sit for a month. TanyaElisabeth 20/12/2024 9:26 am

He gets quite a bit of money and they can’t garnish his wages because he is considered government property because he gets VA benefits 100% VA benefits anyway so you can’t sue the federal government without the federal government permission so that’s kind of where I’m at. So at the end of the day, I have to pursue criminal charges against him because the AG officer is telling me to shit in the corner round room

So he just gets away with not paying that money or? I'm so lost
TanyaElisabeth 20/12/2024 9:28 am
It’s not that he gets away with not paying it’s just especially in the state of Texas, that they allow vets to pretty much get away with murder at this point he is at double if not triple felony charges because of the dollar amount but because it’s the first time I’m bringing this action according what I’m reading onlineI can only go after it for the single charge of felony on payment and it is a felony and he can serve up to six years in jail if convicted of it so they told me I have to basically go file charges. It goes in front of a grand jury and if these indicted, then they can obtain a no knock warrant.

Luna Mae 25/12/2024 9:59 pm
I tried explaining this to my husband and it just now sunk in what I was trying to tell him everyday for the last almost 19 years that we have been together. That i needed something that was just for me outside of our home so that when I was home I could give my all. But because he thought my place was in the home with my children. Even now that they are all grown up, it is and was expected of me to take care of my children. And anytime I worked or went to school or hung out with friends or hell even went to the gym to get loose weight because I was "Fat" he would get upset and we would get into knock out drag out fights over it. If i was out of the house for too long taking care of bills and appointments or running errands he would get upset even though i would take all the of my kids with me and sometimes I would have 3 or 4 more on top of that because I was expected to take care of his brothers and sister's kids too. Anytime I fought back and went and did what I needed to do for myself to stay sane he would make my life a living hell until I quit. It's finally gotten to the point that I've given up on living for the most part. I'm just in survival mode. Anyways sorry for ranting
Luna Mae
I tried explaining this to my husband and it just now sunk in what I was trying to tell him everyday for the last almost 19 years that we have been together. Luna Mae 8/4/2025 7:48 am
A week later and I'm still questioning weather or not i did the right thing by not letting my son move with us. My therapist says he's proud of me because for once in my life I set a boundary and stuck to it even though it was hell going through the backlash from it but i did it. He's now in Texas with his step brother. I hope he can get the help he needs and that he does better with them.
Aki 10/4/2025 1:02 am

Luna Mae 22/4/2025 1:10 am
I think I have a parenting win finally!!!! Ellery had our whole family on his phone bill but we are the ones who actually pay the bill and they each pay us their portion of the bill. Well Gavin wasn't paying his portion of the bill and after several months of doing this Ellery finally forced him off his account and onto his own account. well his first bill comes out this week and he is freaking out because he doesn't have enough to cover it so his account will go into over draft. and for the first time since he started working he is not pulling money out of his future paychecks to get in game credits or buy a new game. He's letting it sit there for as long as possible so that on Wednesday when he goes to pull the money out he will have as much as he can possible get before payday out. ( they have dailypay that allows for them to pull money out for emergancies and the longer they wait the more money the can withdraw up until two days for before paychecks hit accounts) He has not touched the money so far and has made it very clear that he is using this money for his phone bill because he can not go without his phone. We have also made it very clear that we will not help him out with his phone bill. that he is an adult and needs to take care of it himself. I feel like with this bill coming up this week that he is finally getting that we are not playing around and that he needs to step up and take care of himself. I am loving this change for my son and I so badly want him to succeed in doing this one thing right now.
1:12 am
Also Gavin is getting more hours at work and they are going to see if they can start giving him more responsiblity at work durning the slower times of the day to hopefully minimize him getting overwhelmed by the lunch rush or dinner rush.
